network_policies.name: ds-pipeline-metadata-grpc-template-value
issues: policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)

network_policies.name: kserve-controller-manager
issues: ingress rule 1 allows traffic from all sources (no 'from' selector)
ingress rule 1 allows all ports (no port restriction)
no policyTypes specified (defaults to Ingress only, egress is unrestricted)

network_policies.name: mariadb-template-value
issues: policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)

policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)

fields.name: ManagementState
fields.doc: Set to one of the following values: - "Managed" : the operator is actively managing the bundled Argo Workflows controllers. It will only upgrade the Argo Workflows controllers if it is safe to do so. This is the default behavior. - "Removed" : the operator is not managing the bundled Argo Workflows controllers and will not install it. If it is installed, the operator will remove it but will not remove other Argo Workflows installations.

fields.name: RawDeploymentServiceConfig
fields.doc: Configures the type of service that is created for InferenceServices using RawDeployment. The values for RawDeploymentServiceConfig can be "Headless" (default value) or "Headed". Headless: to set "ServiceClusterIPNone = true" in the 'inferenceservice-config' configmap for Kserve. Headed: to set "ServiceClusterIPNone = false" in the 'inferenceservice-config' configmap for Kserve.

api_types.name: DSCInitialization
api_types.doc: DSCInitialization is the Schema for the dscinitializations API.

api_types.name: DSCInitializationList
api_types.doc: DSCInitializationList contains a list of DSCInitialization.

api_types.name: DSCInitializationSpec
api_types.doc: DSCInitializationSpec defines the desired state of DSCInitialization.

fields.name: ApplicationsNamespace
fields.doc: Namespace for applications to be installed, non-configurable, default to "redhat-ods-applications"

fields.name: Monitoring
fields.doc: Enable monitoring on specified namespace

fields.name: ServiceMesh
fields.doc: Configures Service Mesh as networking layer for Data Science Clusters components. The Service Mesh is a mandatory prerequisite for single model serving (KServe) and you should review this configuration if you are planning to use KServe. For other components, it enhances user experience; e.g. it provides unified authentication giving a Single Sign On experience.

fields.name: TrustedCABundle
fields.doc: When set to `Managed`, adds odh-trusted-ca-bundle Configmap to all namespaces that includes cluster-wide Trusted CA Bundle in .data["ca-bundle.crt"]. Additionally, this fields allows admins to add custom CA bundles to the configmap using the .CustomCABundle field.

fields.name: DevFlags
fields.doc: Internal development useful field to test customizations. This is not recommended to be used in production environment.

api_types.name: DevFlags
api_types.doc: DevFlags defines list of fields that can be used by developers to test customizations. This is not recommended to be used in production environment.

fields.name: ManifestsUri
fields.doc: ## DEPRECATED ## : ManifestsUri set on DSCI is not maintained. Custom manifests uri for odh-manifests

fields.name: LogMode
fields.doc: ## DEPRECATED ##: Ignored, use LogLevel instead

fields.name: LogLevel
fields.doc: Override Zap log level. Can be "debug", "info", "error" or a number (more verbose).

fields.name: ManagementState
fields.doc: managementState indicates whether and how the operator should manage customized CA bundle

fields.name: CustomCABundle
fields.doc: A custom CA bundle that will be available for  all  components in the Data Science Cluster(DSC). This bundle will be stored in odh-trusted-ca-bundle ConfigMap .data.odh-ca-bundle.crt .

api_types.name: DSCInitializationStatus
api_types.doc: DSCInitializationStatus defines the observed state of DSCInitialization.

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Phase describes the Phase of DSCInitializationStatus This is used by OLM UI to provide status information to the user

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions describes the state of the DSCInitializationStatus resource

fields.name: RelatedObjects
fields.doc: RelatedObjects is a list of objects created and maintained by this operator. Object references will be added to this list after they have been created AND found in the cluster

fields.name: Release
fields.doc: Version and release type

fields.name: TrustedCABundle
fields.doc: When set to `Managed`, adds odh-trusted-ca-bundle Configmap to all namespaces that includes cluster-wide Trusted CA Bundle in .data["ca-bundle.crt"]. Additionally, this fields allows admins to add custom CA bundles to the configmap using the .CustomCABundle field.

fields.name: DevFlags
fields.doc: Internal development useful field to test customizations. This is not recommended to be used in production environment.

api_types.name: DevFlags
api_types.doc: DevFlags defines list of fields that can be used by developers to test customizations. This is not recommended to be used in production environment.

fields.name: LogLevel
fields.doc: Override Zap log level. Can be "debug", "info", "error" or a number (more verbose).

fields.name: ManagementState
fields.doc: managementState indicates whether and how the operator should manage customized CA bundle

fields.name: CustomCABundle
fields.doc: A custom CA bundle that will be available for  all  components in the Data Science Cluster(DSC). This bundle will be stored in odh-trusted-ca-bundle ConfigMap .data.odh-ca-bundle.crt .

api_types.name: DSCInitializationStatus
api_types.doc: DSCInitializationStatus defines the observed state of DSCInitialization.

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Phase describes the Phase of DSCInitializationStatus This is used by OLM UI to provide status information to the user

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Conditions describes the state of the DSCInitializationStatus resource

fields.name: RelatedObjects
fields.doc: RelatedObjects is a list of objects created and maintained by this operator. Object references will be added to this list after they have been created AND found in the cluster

fields.name: Release
fields.doc: Version and release type

api_types.name: FeatureTracker
api_types.doc: FeatureTracker represents a cluster-scoped resource in the Data Science Cluster, specifically designed for monitoring and managing objects created via the internal Features API. This resource serves a crucial role in cross-namespace resource management, acting as an owner reference for various resources. The primary purpose of the FeatureTracker is to enable efficient garbage collection by Kubernetes. This is essential for ensuring that resources are automatically cleaned up and reclaimed when they are no longer required.

api_types.name: Source
api_types.doc: Source describes the type of object that created the related Feature to this FeatureTracker.

api_types.name: FeatureTrackerSpec
api_types.doc: FeatureTrackerSpec defines the desired state of FeatureTracker.

api_types.name: FeatureTrackerStatus
api_types.doc: FeatureTrackerStatus defines the observed state of FeatureTracker.

fields.name: Phase
fields.doc: Phase describes the Phase of FeatureTracker reconciliation state. This is used by OLM UI to provide status information to the user.

api_types.name: FeatureTrackerList
api_types.doc: FeatureTrackerList contains a list of FeatureTracker.

api_types.name: CertificateSpec
api_types.doc: CertificateSpec represents the specification of the certificate securing communications of an Istio Gateway.

fields.name: SecretName
fields.doc: SecretName specifies the name of the Kubernetes Secret resource that contains a TLS certificate secure HTTP communications for the KNative network.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type specifies if the TLS certificate should be generated automatically, or if the certificate is provided by the user. Allowed values are: * SelfSigned: A certificate is going to be generated using an own private key. * Provided: Pre-existence of the TLS Secret (see SecretName) with a valid certificate is assumed. * OpenshiftDefaultIngress: Default ingress certificate configured for OpenShift

api_types.name: NodeSchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: NodeSchedulingSpec defines direct node scheduling configuration.

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector specifies the node selector to use for direct node scheduling. Workloads will be scheduled only on nodes that match all the specified labels.

fields.name: Tolerations
fields.doc: Tolerations specifies the tolerations to apply to workloads for direct node scheduling. These tolerations allow workloads to be scheduled on nodes with matching taints.

api_types.name: HardwareProfile
api_types.doc: HardwareProfile is the Schema for the hardwareprofiles API.

api_types.name: HardwareProfileList
api_types.doc: HardwareProfileList contains a list of HardwareProfile.

api_types.name: HardwareProfileSpec
api_types.doc: HardwareProfileSpec defines the desired state of HardwareProfile.

fields.name: Identifiers
fields.doc: The array of identifiers

fields.name: SchedulingSpec
fields.doc: SchedulingSpec specifies how workloads using this hardware profile should be scheduled.

fields.name: DisplayName
fields.doc: The display name of identifier.

fields.name: Identifier
fields.doc: The resource identifier of the hardware device.

fields.name: MinCount
fields.doc: The minimum count can be an integer or a string.

fields.name: MaxCount
fields.doc: The maximum count can be an integer or a string.

fields.name: DefaultCount
fields.doc: The default count can be an integer or a string.

fields.name: ResourceType
fields.doc: The type of identifier. could be "CPU", "Memory", or "Accelerator". Leave it undefined for the other types.

api_types.name: SchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: SchedulingSpec allows for specifying either kueue-based scheduling or direct node scheduling. CEL Rule 1: If schedulingType is "Queue", the 'kueue' field (with a non-empty localQueueName) must be set, and the 'node' field must not be set. CEL Rule 2: If schedulingType is "Node", the 'node' field must be set, and the 'kueue' field must not be set.

fields.name: SchedulingType
fields.doc: SchedulingType is the scheduling method discriminator. Users must set this value to indicate which scheduling method to use. The value of this field should match exactly one configured scheduling method. Valid values are "Queue" and "Node".

fields.name: Kueue
fields.doc: Kueue specifies queue-based scheduling configuration. This field is only valid when schedulingType is "Queue".

fields.name: Node
fields.doc: node specifies direct node scheduling configuration. This field is only valid when schedulingType is "Node".

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.type == 'Queue' ? (has(self.kueue) && has(self.kueue.localQueueName) && !has(self.node)) : true",message="When schedulingType is 'Queue', the 'kueue.localQueueName' field must be specified and non-empty, and the 'node' field must not be set"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.type == 'Node' ? (has(self.node) && !has(self.kueue)) : true",message="When schedulingType is 'Node', the 'node' field must be set, and the 'kueue' field must not be set"

api_types.name: KueueSchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: KueueSchedulingSpec defines queue-based scheduling configuration.

fields.name: LocalQueueName
fields.doc: LocalQueueName specifies the name of the local queue to use for workload scheduling. When specified, workloads using this hardware profile will be submitted to the specified queue and the queue's configuration will determine the actual node placement and tolerations.

fields.name: PriorityClass
fields.doc: PriorityClass specifies the name of the WorkloadPriorityClass associated with the HardwareProfile.

api_types.name: ServingSpec
api_types.doc: ServingSpec specifies the configuration for the KNative Serving components and their bindings with the Service Mesh.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name specifies the name of the KNativeServing resource that is going to be created to instruct the KNative Operator to deploy KNative serving components. This resource is created in the "knative-serving" namespace.

fields.name: IngressGateway
fields.doc: IngressGateway allows to customize some parameters for the Istio Ingress Gateway that is bound to KNative-Serving.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is a name Service Mesh Control Plane. Defaults to "data-science-smcp".

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: Namespace is a namespace where Service Mesh is deployed. Defaults to "istio-system".

fields.name: MetricsCollection
fields.doc: MetricsCollection specifies if metrics from components on the Mesh namespace should be collected. Setting the value to "Istio" will collect metrics from the control plane and any proxies on the Mesh namespace (like gateway pods). Setting to "None" will disable metrics collection.

api_types.name: GatewaySpec
api_types.doc: GatewaySpec represents the configuration of the Ingress Gateways.

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain specifies the host name for intercepting incoming requests. Most likely, you will want to use a wildcard name, like *.example.com. If not set, the domain of the OpenShift Ingress is used. If you choose to generate a certificate, this is the domain used for the certificate request.

fields.name: Certificate
fields.doc: Certificate specifies configuration of the TLS certificate securing communication for the gateway.

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: Namespace where it is deployed. If not provided, the default is to use '-auth-provider' suffix on the ApplicationsNamespace of the DSCI.

fields.name: Audiences
fields.doc: Audiences is a list of the identifiers that the resource server presented with the token identifies as. Audience-aware token authenticators will verify that the token was intended for at least one of the audiences in this list. If no audiences are provided, the audience will default to the audience of the Kubernetes apiserver (kubernetes.default.svc).

api_types.name: ServiceMeshSpec
api_types.doc: ServiceMeshSpec configures Service Mesh.

fields.name: ControlPlane
fields.doc: ControlPlane holds configuration of Service Mesh used by Opendatahub.

fields.name: Auth
fields.doc: Auth holds configuration of authentication and authorization services used by Service Mesh in Opendatahub.

api_types.name: NodeSchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: NodeSchedulingSpec defines direct node scheduling configuration.

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector specifies the node selector to use for direct node scheduling. Workloads will be scheduled only on nodes that match all the specified labels.

fields.name: Tolerations
fields.doc: Tolerations specifies the tolerations to apply to workloads for direct node scheduling. These tolerations allow workloads to be scheduled on nodes with matching taints.

api_types.doc: HardwareProfile is the Schema for the hardwareprofiles API.

api_types.name: HardwareProfileList
api_types.doc: HardwareProfileList contains a list of HardwareProfile.

api_types.name: HardwareProfileSpec
api_types.doc: HardwareProfileSpec defines the desired state of HardwareProfile.

fields.name: SchedulingSpec

fields.name: DisplayName

fields.name: Identifier

fields.name: MinCount
fields.doc: The minimum count can be an integer or a string.

fields.name: MaxCount
fields.doc: The maximum count can be an integer or a string.

fields.name: DefaultCount
fields.doc: The default count can be an integer or a string.

fields.name: ResourceType
fields.doc: The type of identifier. could be "CPU", "Memory", or "Accelerator". Leave it undefined for the other types.

api_types.doc: SchedulingSpec allows for specifying either kueue-based scheduling or direct node scheduling. CEL Rule 1: If schedulingType is "Queue", the 'kueue' field (with a non-empty localQueueName) must be set, and the 'node' field must not be set. CEL Rule 2: If schedulingType is "Node", the 'node' field must be set, and the 'kueue' field must not be set.

fields.name: SchedulingType
fields.doc: SchedulingType is the scheduling method discriminator. Users must set this value to indicate which scheduling method to use. The value of this field should match exactly one configured scheduling method. Valid values are "Queue" and "Node".

fields.name: Kueue
fields.doc: Kueue specifies queue-based scheduling configuration. This field is only valid when schedulingType is "Queue".

fields.name: Node
fields.doc: node specifies direct node scheduling configuration. This field is only valid when schedulingType is "Node".

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.type == 'Queue' ? (has(self.kueue) && has(self.kueue.localQueueName) && !has(self.node)) : true",message="When schedulingType is 'Queue', the 'kueue.localQueueName' field must be specified and non-empty, and the 'node' field must not be set"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.type == 'Node' ? (has(self.node) && !has(self.kueue)) : true",message="When schedulingType is 'Node', the 'node' field must be set, and the 'kueue' field must not be set"

api_types.name: KueueSchedulingSpec
api_types.doc: KueueSchedulingSpec defines queue-based scheduling configuration.

fields.name: LocalQueueName
fields.doc: LocalQueueName specifies the name of the local queue to use for workload scheduling. When specified, workloads using this hardware profile will be submitted to the specified queue and the queue's configuration will determine the actual node placement and tolerations.

fields.name: PriorityClass
fields.doc: PriorityClass specifies the name of the WorkloadPriorityClass associated with the HardwareProfile.

api_types.name: AuthSpec
api_types.doc: AuthSpec defines the desired state of Auth

fields.name: AdminGroups
fields.doc: AdminGroups cannot contain 'system:authenticated' (security risk) or empty strings, and must not be empty
markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.all(group, group != 'system:authenticated' && group != '')",message="AdminGroups cannot contain 'system:authenticated' or empty strings"

fields.name: AllowedGroups
fields.doc: AllowedGroups cannot contain empty strings, but 'system:authenticated' is allowed for general access

api_types.name: AuthStatus
api_types.doc: AuthStatus defines the observed state of Auth

api_types.name: Auth
api_types.doc: Auth is the Schema for the auths API

api_types.name: AuthList
api_types.doc: AuthList contains a list of Auth

api_types.name: GatewayConfigStatus
api_types.doc: GatewayConfigStatus defines the observed state of GatewayConfig

api_types.name: GatewayConfig
api_types.doc: GatewayConfig is the Schema for the gatewayconfigs API

api_types.name: GatewayConfigList
api_types.doc: GatewayConfigList contains a list of GatewayConfig

api_types.name: GatewayConfigSpec
api_types.doc: GatewayConfigSpec defines the desired state of GatewayConfig

fields.name: OIDC
fields.doc: OIDC configuration (used when cluster is in OIDC authentication mode)

fields.name: Certificate
fields.doc: Certificate management

fields.name: Domain
fields.doc: Domain configuration for the GatewayConfig Example: apps.example.com

fields.name: Cookie
fields.doc: Cookie configuration for OAuth2 proxy (applies to both OIDC and OpenShift OAuth)

fields.name: AuthTimeout
fields.doc: AuthTimeout is the duration Envoy waits for auth proxy responses. Requests timeout with 403 if exceeded. Overrides GATEWAY_AUTH_TIMEOUT env var. Default: "5s"

api_types.name: OIDCConfig
api_types.doc: OIDCConfig defines OIDC provider configuration

fields.name: IssuerURL
fields.doc: OIDC issuer URL

fields.name: ClientID
fields.doc: OIDC client ID

fields.name: ClientSecretRef
fields.doc: Reference to secret containing client secret

api_types.name: CookieConfig
api_types.doc: CookieConfig defines cookie settings for OAuth2 proxy

fields.name: Expire
fields.doc: Expire duration for OAuth2 proxy session cookie (e.g., "24h", "8h") This controls how long the session cookie is valid before requiring re-authentication. Default: "24h"

fields.name: Refresh
fields.doc: Refresh duration for OAuth2 proxy to refresh access tokens (e.g., "2h", "1h", "30m") This must be LESS than the OIDC provider's Access Token Lifespan to avoid token expiration. For example, if Keycloak Access Token Lifespan is 1 hour, set this to "30m" or "45m". Default: "1h"

api_types.name: TracesTLS
api_types.doc: TracesTLS defines TLS configuration for traces collection

fields.name: Enabled
fields.doc: Enabled enables TLS for Tempo gRPC connections

fields.name: CertificateSecret
fields.doc: CertificateSecret specifies the name of the secret containing TLS certificates If not specified, OpenShift service serving certificates will be used

fields.name: CAConfigMap
fields.doc: CAConfigMap specifies the name of the ConfigMap containing the CA certificate Required for mutual TLS authentication

api_types.name: TracesStorage
api_types.doc: TracesStorage defines the storage configuration for tracing.

fields.name: Backend
fields.doc: Backend defines the storage backend type. Valid values are "pv", "s3", and "gcs".

fields.name: Size
fields.doc: Size specifies the size of the storage. This field is optional.

fields.name: Secret
fields.doc: Secret specifies the secret name for storage credentials. This field is required when the backend is not "pv".

fields.name: Retention
fields.doc: Retention specifies how long trace data should be retained globally (e.g., "60m", "10h")

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.backend != 'pv' ? has(self.secret) : true", message="When backend is s3 or gcs, the 'secret' field must be specified and non-empty"
+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.backend != 'pv' ? !has(self.size) : true", message="Size is supported when backend is pv only"

api_types.name: Monitoring
api_types.doc: Monitoring is the Schema for the monitorings API

api_types.name: MonitoringCommonSpec
api_types.doc: MonitoringCommonSpec spec defines the shared desired state of Dashboard

fields.name: Namespace
fields.doc: monitoring spec exposed to DSCI api Namespace for monitoring if it is enabled

fields.name: Metrics
fields.doc: metrics collection

fields.name: Traces
fields.doc: Tracing configuration for OpenTelemetry instrumentation

fields.name: Alerting
fields.doc: Alerting configuration for Prometheus

fields.name: CollectorReplicas
fields.doc: CollectorReplicas specifies the number of replicas in opentelemetry-collector, default is 2 if not set

markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="has(self.alerting) ? has(self.metrics.storage) || has(self.metrics.resources) : true",message="Alerting configuration requires metrics.storage or metrics.resources to be configured"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="!has(self.collectorReplicas) || (self.collectorReplicas > 0 && ((self.metrics.resources != null || self.metrics.storage != null) || self.traces != null))",message="CollectorReplicas can only be set when metrics.resources, metrics.storage or traces are configured, and must be > 0"

api_types.name: MonitoringList
api_types.doc: MonitoringList contains a list of Monitoring

fields.name: ManagementSpec
fields.doc: configuration fields common across services

fields.name: MonitoringCommonSpec
fields.doc: monitoring specific fields

api_types.name: MonitoringSpec
api_types.doc: MonitoringSpec defines the desired state of Monitoring

fields.name: MonitoringCommonSpec
fields.doc: monitoring spec exposed to DSCI api

api_types.name: Metrics
api_types.doc: Metrics defines the desired state of metrics for the monitoring service

fields.name: Replicas
fields.doc: Replicas specifies the number of replicas in monitoringstack. If not set, it defaults to 1 on single-node clusters and 2 on multi-node clusters.

fields.name: Exporters
fields.doc: Exporters defines custom metrics exporters for sending metrics to external observability tools. Each key-value pair represents an exporter name and its configuration. Reserved names 'prometheus' and 'otlp/tempo' cannot be used as they conflict with built-in exporters.
markers: +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="!('prometheus' in self)",message="exporter name 'prometheus' is reserved and cannot be used"
+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="!('otlp/tempo' in self)",message="exporter name 'otlp/tempo' is reserved and cannot be used"
+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="self.all(k, self[k] != '')",message="exporter configuration values must be non-empty strings"

+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation:rule="!(self.storage == null && self.resources == null) || !has(self.replicas) || self.replicas == 0",message="Replicas can only be set to non-zero value when either Storage or Resources is configured"

api_types.name: MetricsStorage
api_types.doc: MetricsStorage defines the storage configuration for the monitoring service

fields.name: Size
fields.doc: Size specifies the storage size for the MonitoringStack (e.g, "5Gi", "10Mi")

fields.name: Retention
fields.doc: Retention specifies how long metrics data should be retained (e.g., "1d", "2w")

api_types.name: MetricsResources
api_types.doc: MetricsResources defines the resource requests and limits for the monitoring service

fields.name: CPULimit
fields.doc: CPULimit specifies the maximum CPU allocation (e.g., "500m", "2")

fields.name: MemoryLimit
fields.doc: MemoryLimit specifies the maximum memory allocation (e.g., "1Gi", "512Mi")

fields.name: CPURequest
fields.doc: CPURequest specifies the minimum CPU allocation (e.g., "100m", "0.5")

fields.name: MemoryRequest
fields.doc: MemoryRequest specifies the minimum memory allocation (e.g., "256Mi", "1Gi")

api_types.name: MonitoringStatus
api_types.doc: MonitoringStatus defines the observed state of Monitoring

api_types.name: Traces
api_types.doc: Traces enables and defines the configuration for traces collection

fields.name: SampleRatio
fields.doc: SampleRatio determines the sampling rate for traces Value should be between 0.0 (no sampling) and 1.0 (sample all traces)

fields.name: TLS
fields.doc: TLS configuration for Tempo gRPC connections

fields.name: Exporters
fields.doc: Exporters defines custom trace exporters for sending traces to external observability tools. Each key represents the exporter name, and the value contains the exporter configuration. The configuration follows the OpenTelemetry Collector exporter format.

operator_config.name: dashboardPath
operator_config.doc: Dashboard path on the gateway.

operator_config.name: sectionTitle
LegacyComponentName
operator_config.doc:
LegacyComponentName is the name of the component that is assigned to deployments
via Kustomize. Since a deployment selector is immutable, we can't upgrade existing
deployment to the new component name, so keep it around till we figure out a solution.

operator_config.name: ReadyConditionType
operator_config.doc:
ReadyConditionType is the condition type for AIPipelines in v2 (storage version).
The conversion webhook will translate this to DataSciencePipelinesReady for v1 users.

operator_config.name: finalizerName
operator_config.doc: TODO: remove after https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHOAIENG-15920

operator_config.name: adminGroups
operator_config.doc:
adminGroups maps each supported platform to its default admin group name.
These groups are assigned administrative privileges in the Auth custom resource.

Platform mappings:
  - SelfManagedRhoai: "rhods-admins" - for self-managed Red Hat OpenShift AI
  - ManagedRhoai: "dedicated-admins" - for managed/hosted Red Hat OpenShift AI
  - OpenDataHub: "odh-admins" - for Open Data Hub deployments

The admin group members have full administrative access to the platform,
including the ability to manage users, configure components, and access
all platform resources.

operator_config.name: AuthClientID
operator_config.doc: Authentication constants.

operator_config.name: AuthProxyHTTPPort

operator_config.name: AlertingNotConfiguredMessage
operator_config.value: Alerting not configured in DSCI CR

operator_config.name: AuthProxyExternalAuthNoDeploymentMessage
operator_config.value: Cluster uses external authentication, no gateway auth proxy deployed

operator_config.name: AuthProxyFailedCallbackRouteMessage
operator_config.value: Failed to create auth callback route

operator_config.name: AuthProxyOIDCModeWithoutConfigMessage

operator_config.name: COOMissingMessage
operator_config.value: ClusterObservability operator must be installed for metrics configuration

operator_config.name: CodeFlarePresentMessage
operator_config.value:
Failed upgrade: CodeFlare component is present in the cluster. It must be uninstalled to proceed with Ray component upgrade.
To uninstall it, you should delete all RayClusters resources from the cluster, delete the CodeFlare component resource and recreate the RayClusters.
operator_config.doc: For v3 upgrade sanity checks.

operator_config.name: DataSciencePipelinesArgoWorkflowsCRDMissingMessage
operator_config.value: Argo Workflows controllers are not managed by this operator, but the CRD is missing

operator_config.name: DataSciencePipelinesArgoWorkflowsNotManagedMessage
operator_config.value: Argo Workflows controllers are not managed by this operator

operator_config.name: ISVCMissingCRDMessage

operator_config.name: KueueOperatorNotInstalledMessage
operator_config.value: Kueue operator not installed, install it or change kueue component state to Managed

operator_config.name: KueueStateManagedNotSupportedMessage
operator_config.value: Kueue managementState Managed is not supported, please use Removed or Unmanaged

operator_config.name: MetricsNotConfiguredMessage

operator_config.name: MultiKueueCRDMessage
operator_config.value: Kueue CRDs MultiKueueConfig v1alpha1 and/or MultiKueueCluster v1alpha1 exist, please remove them to proceed

operator_config.name: OpenTelemetryCollectorOperatorMissingMessage
operator_config.value: OpenTelemetryCollector operator must be installed for OpenTelemetry configuration

operator_config.name: PhaseClusterExpanding
operator_config.doc:
PhaseClusterExpanding is used when cluster is expanding capacity
is an example of a constant that is not used anywhere in the code.

operator_config.name: PhaseConnecting
operator_config.doc:
PhaseConnecting is used when cluster is connecting to external cluster
is an example of a constant that is not used anywhere in the code.

operator_config.name: PhaseDeleting
operator_config.doc:
PhaseDeleting is used when cluster is deleting
is an example of a constant that is not used anywhere in the code.

operator_config.name: PhaseError
operator_config.doc: PhaseError is used when SetErrorCondition() is called.

operator_config.name: PhaseIgnored
operator_config.doc:
PhaseIgnored is used when a resource is ignored
is an example of a constant that is not used anywhere in the code.

operator_config.name: PhaseOnboarding
operator_config.doc:
PhaseOnboarding is used when consumer is Onboarding
is an example of a constant that is not used anywhere in the code.

operator_config.name: ReconcileFailed
operator_config.doc: ReconcileFailed is used when multiple DSCI instance exists or DSC reconcile failed/removal failed.

operator_config.name: TempoOperatorMissingMessage
